name: Fremantle (Perth)
summary: When your ship ties up at Fremantle, you've arrived at Perth's salty seaside doorway. Wander 'Freo's' old harbour streets, hop a 30-minute train into sunny Perth city, or jump on a fast ferry to Rottnest Island where smiley quokkas live. One day, three big choices!
funFacts: Rottnest Island is home to about 10,000 quokkas, fluffy hopping marsupials famous as the world's happiest-looking animal and superstars of selfies.
Fremantle Prison was built by convicts in the 1850s using their own hands, and bad guys were locked inside for 136 years until 1991.
Perth is Australia's sunniest capital city, basking in roughly 3,200 hours of sunshine and almost 139 totally clear days every year.
The Swan River got its name from Dutch sailors in 1697 who were astonished to spot black swans there, instead of white ones like back home.
Just south of Fremantle, Penguin Island shelters around 200 wild bottlenose dolphins offshore plus a colony of tiny Little Penguins that come ashore at night.
thingsToDo: Climb aboard the HMAS Ovens submarine at the WA Maritime Museum in Fremantle and squeeze through narrow hatches like a real Cold War sailor.
Take the 'Escape from Fremantle Prison' kids tour, helping cheeky Moondyne Joe Junior plot a getaway through the convict-built corridors.
Catch the fast Rottnest Express ferry from Fremantle, hire bikes on the island, and try snapping a respectful quokka selfie from two metres away.
Hop the Transperth Fremantle Line train 30 minutes into Perth, then ride the elevator up Kings Park's treetop walkway for huge Swan River views.
Browse the buzzing Fremantle Markets for snacks, magic tricks and street performers, then climb the spinning Fremantle Tourist Wheel by Esplanade Park.
Visit the historic Round House (1831) at the top of Arthur Head and watch the One O'Clock Gun thunder out across the Indian Ocean.

localWord.meaning: 'Hello' in the Noongar language, spoken by the Whadjuk people who have cared for the Perth and Fremantle area for tens of thousands of years.

images.alt: Perth's shiny city skyline rises above the Swan River at Elizabeth Quay.
Three big cruise ships are docked side by side at Fremantle's busy harbour.
The tall stone gatehouse of historic Fremantle Prison stands under a bright blue sky.
A smiling little quokka peeks up close at the camera on Rottnest Island.
Soft white sand and turquoise waves stretch along sunny Cottesloe Beach.
